data:
P  R  E  O  N  E    E  N  T  E  R  P  R  I  S  E
PreOne Enterprise
API Contract Catalog
Enterprise Preschool Operating System
Document: API Contract Catalog v1.0
Status: API Freeze
Date: 2026-07-12
Predecessor: Vision/BRC/PRD/DDD/ERD v1.0
Successor: Backend Implementation + Mobile + Web + OpenAPI + SDK
Scope: 14 domains, ~530 REST APIs, 5 WebSocket channels, 5 webhook events
Classification: Internal Engineering Reference
Prepared by: PreOne Product & Architecture Team

PreOne Platform                                        API Contract v1.0
Strategic vision, AI-first platform direction, target users, KPIs
176 business rules across 12 categories — every API enforces ≥1 rule
30 BRs + 60 FRs — each FR maps to ≥1 API endpoint
8 bounded contexts, 15 aggregates — every API call hits exactly 1 aggregate
307 tables — every API payload maps to specific tables/columns
Architecture Decision Records — every API standard references relevant ADR
All 30 chapters drafted; domain catalogs added; review
API Freeze — signed off by Governance Board
UI  →  REST API  →  Application Service  →  DDD Aggregate  →  Repository  →  Database
Resource-based URLs, standard HTTP methods, JSON payloads. No RPC-style endpoints unless absolutely necessary (e.g., /admissions/{id}/approve is an exception pattern for state transitions).
Request and response bodies are always application/json. No XML, no form-encoded bodies (except multipart/form-data for file uploads).
Every request contains all context (auth, tenant, locale). Server does not maintain session state between requests. Enables horizontal scaling.
URLs name resources (nouns), not actions (verbs). Plural nouns: /students, /invoices, /attendance. Sub-resources: /students/{id}/guardians.
URI path versioning /api/v1/, /api/v2/. Backward-compatible changes within a major version. Breaking changes require new major version with 12-month overlap.
POST/PUT/PATCH support Idempotency-Key header. Same key + same payload returns cached response. Mandatory for payment, admission, file upload endpoints.
TLS 1.3 mandatory. JWT 15min access, 30day refresh. PII encrypted at rest (pgcrypto). Rate-limited per IP + per user. DPDP Act compliance built-in.
Every request scoped via X-Tenant-ID, X-School-ID, X-Branch-ID headers. Row-level security enforced at DB layer. No cross-tenant data access without explicit platform-admin scope.
Spec is source of truth. NestJS swagger decorators generate openapi.json. Redoc renders docs. Postman collection auto-generated. SDK auto-generated for TypeScript/Python.
Endpoints accept machine-readable context (X-Request-ID, traceId). Consistent error codes (DOMAIN_REASON). Predictable pagination. LLM agents can navigate the API without human curation.
Developer machine, hot-reload, ngrok for webhooks
TLS 1.3 mandatory (TLS 1.2 deprecated, phased out by 2026-12). HSTS preload. Perfect forward secrecy. OCSP stapling.
JWT 15min access token, 30day refresh token, rotated on refresh. API Key (long-lived) for service-to-service. Service Token for internal microservices via mTLS.
RBAC + ABAC hybrid. Permission Bundles (predefined role-based). Scope Resolution (school/branch/class/section). Effective Access Resolver caches resolved permissions.
class-validator on NestJS DTOs. Whitelist allowed fields. Reject unknown fields. Sanitize HTML via DOMPurify. SQL injection prevented via Prisma parameterized queries.
JSON serialization escapes HTML entities by default. No reflected XSS surface. CDN adds Content-Security-Policy headers.
Per-IP (60 req/min anon, 600 req/min auth). Per-user (600 req/min). Per-tenant (10k req/min). 429 with Retry-After header. Cloudflare DDoS in front.
HashiCorp Vault stores DB credentials, JWT signing keys, third-party API keys. Rotated quarterly. No secrets in env files or git.
Every state-changing request logged to audit_log table (user, tenant, endpoint, payload-hash, response-status, timestamp). Retained 7 years per DPDP §31.
PII fields encrypted at rest (pgcrypto). Data Subject Access Requests (DSAR) endpoint. Consent withdrawal triggers cascade delete. Breach notification within 72h per §17.
Outbound webhooks signed with HMAC-SHA256. X-PreOne-Signature header. Recipient verifies signature. Replay attack prevention via timestamp window (5min).
Allow-list origins (preone.in, *.preone.in, preonehub.in). Credentials allowed. Preflight cache 24h. No wildcard origins in production.

All 10 vulnerabilities mitigated: A01 Broken Access Control (RBAC+ABAC), A02 Crypto Failures (TLS 1.3 + pgcrypto), A03 Injection (Prisma param queries), A04 Insecure Design (threat modeling per feature), A05 Misconfig (CIS benchmarks), A06 Vulnerable Components (Dependabot), A07 Auth Failures (MFA optional), A08 Data Integrity (signed webhooks), A09 Logging (audit_log + Sentry), A10 SSRF (egress allowlist).
POST /auth/login with {mobile, otp}. OTP sent via SMS (MSG91). 6-digit, 5min expiry, 3 retries. Returns accessToken + refreshToken.
POST /auth/login with {email, password}. bcrypt-hashed password. Optional MFA via TOTP (Google Authenticator). Account lockout after 5 failed attempts.
POST /auth/refresh with {refreshToken}. Issues new accessToken. Old refresh token rotated (one-time use). 30-day sliding window.
Bearer token in Authorization header. 15min expiry. RS256 signed. Payload: user_id, tenant_id, roles, permissions_version, scope_version, iat, exp.
Long-lived (1 year) key for service-to-service. X-API-Key header. Scoped to specific resources. Revocable via admin UI. Rate-limited per key.
mTLS-secured internal service identity. No user context. Used by background jobs, cron tasks, internal sync. Issued by SPIFFE workload registry.
Internal microservices, cron jobs, queue workers
Bearer JWT  →  Permission Resolver  →  Scope Resolver  →  Application Service
View student profile, guardian info, medical history
Create invoices, record payments, issue refunds
Mark daily attendance, edit same-day attendance
Generate term report cards via AI + manual review
Create/disable users, assign roles, reset passwords
Configure fee heads, academic year, holiday calendar
Branch identifier within school (*required for branch-scoped APIs)
Active academic year (*required for academic APIs)
Response locale (mr, hi, en, mr-IN, hi-IN)
Auto-generated if missing, propagated to all downstream
Client app version for compatibility checks
UUID for safe retry of POST/PUT/PATCH
Retrieve resource(s). Never mutates state. Cacheable. Idempotent.
Create new resource. Trigger state transition. Supports Idempotency-Key.
Partial update of existing resource. Supports Idempotency-Key.
Full replacement of existing resource. Supports Idempotency-Key.
Successful POST that created a new resource
Valid JWT but insufficient permission or scope
Resource does not exist or out of scope
Authentication failures (missing token, expired, invalid signature, revoked)
Input validation failures (required field missing, format invalid, length exceeded)
Authorization failures (insufficient role, out of scope, feature disabled)
Resource not found (id doesn't exist, soft-deleted, out of tenant scope)
State conflicts (duplicate, concurrent update, transition not allowed)
Internal server errors (DB down, 3rd-party timeout, unhandled exception)
Token absent, wrong scheme (Basic instead of Bearer)
Token signature invalid, expired, or revoked
Request body or query param failed validation
Required field missing, format mismatch, length exceeded
User lacks required permission or scope
Student ID doesn't exist or out of tenant scope
Resource conflict — duplicate or state transition blocked
Duplicate admission application, optimistic lock failure
Rate limit exceeded for this client
Scheduled downtime, blue-green deploy in progress
Fetch specific resources by ID list
Sort by lastName asc, then createdAt desc
Current version, full support, bug fixes, new backward-compatible features
Superseded by newer major version. Still functional. Sunset header sent.
End-of-life. Returns 410 Gone. Migration guide published 12 months prior.
Internal microservices via API Key or mTLS
Enterprise tier tenants with high-volume operations
Token-based quota — 10k tokens/day per tenant by default
Total requests allowed in current window
Seconds to wait before retrying (only on 429)
"message": "Rate limit exceeded. Try again in 30 seconds."
Login via OTP or password — returns JWT tokens
R-PLT-003 (Login Rate Limit), R-DAT-004 (Failed Login Lockout)
Rotate refresh token, issue new access token
Get current authenticated user profile + permissions
Update own profile (name, avatar, locale, timezone)
List all permissions available in the system
List all roles defined for the tenant
{ "success": true, "data": [/* role objects with permission bundles */] }
List students with pagination, filtering, sorting
Get single student by ID — full profile
R-ELG-002 (Age Eligibility), R-ELG-005 (Mandatory Docs), R-ADM-007 (Sibling Concession)
Soft-delete student (sets deleted_at, retains for 7y per DPDP)
List admission applications with status filter
R-ELG-001 (Age Eligibility), R-ELG-002 (Doc Checklist), R-ADM-007 (Sibling Concession)
Approve application — generates offer letter + fee quote
Upload admission documents (birth cert, photos, medical)
Mark daily attendance for a class (bulk)
Edit attendance (same-day by teacher, post-day by Branch Head)
{ "success": true, "data": [/* report rows with present/absent counts */] }
List observations for a student/date range
Edit observation (same-day only, post-approval required after)
Get fee structure for academic year / class / branch
Record payment against invoice — Idempotency-Key MANDATORY
R-FIN-010 (Payment Validation), R-FIN-011 (Duplicate Detection), R-FIN-015 (Receipt Generation)
Initiate refund against a successful payment
Send 1:1 message from teacher to parent
List messages for current user (teacher or parent)
List in-app notifications for current user
Goods Received Note — close PO, update stock
Current stock levels with reorder alerts
{ "success": true, "data": [/* stock items with currentQty, reorderLevel */] }
R-HR-001 (BGV Mandatory), R-HR-003 (Qualification Check), R-HR-005 (POSH Training Init)
R-HR-008 (Leave Balance Check), R-HR-009 (Leave Approval Matrix)
Run payroll for month (cron or manual trigger)
R-HR-012 (Payroll Lock), R-FIN-025 (Statutory Deductions), R-HR-015 (Payslip Generation)
{ "success": true, "data": [/* rows with present%, absent%, late% */] }
Real-time 1:1 and group chat between parents and teachers
Live attendance updates to dashboard as teachers mark
Push in-app notifications to user dashboards in real-time
Live KPI updates on branch/school dashboard widgets
School bus live tracking for parents during transit
Admission application approved by Branch Head
Daily attendance marked for a class
Auto-route to fallback on 5xx or timeout >10s
If MSG91 down, retry via Twilio (costlier)
Auto-fallback to SMS if WhatsApp delivery fails
Queue + retry via SES on SendGrid 5xx
Manual attendance entry if device offline
Lesson plan gen, report card draft, observation NLP
Failover to secondary provider; cache common queries
Multi-region replication; R2 as read CDN
Manual document upload if eKYC offline
Virus scan (ClamAV), OCR for searchable text
Virus scan, convert to PDF for preview
Virus scan, resize variants (thumb, medium, large)
Virus scan, EXIF strip, resize variants
Virus scan, transcode to HLS, thumbnail extraction
PII redaction pre-call, output validation, human review before classroom use
MANDATORY teacher review + edit before parent send. Output flagged 'AI DRAFT'.
PII redaction, output schema validation, no human review needed (low-stakes)
{ "rawText": "Aarav today shared his toys with new student. Very patient." }
Bilingual context preserved, teacher MUST edit+send (never auto-send)
{ "parentMessage": "Aarav काल घरी आल्यावर खूप रडला. काही झालं का?", "context": "Yesterday was his first day" }
Only aggregate anonymized data, no individual PII
{ "insights": [{ "type": "TREND", "text": "Attendance dipped on Fridays — possible weekend-effect pattern" }] }
Pre-approved WhatsApp template per use case
New student record created via admission completion
Admission application approved by Branch Head
Daily attendance marked for a class by teacher
New invoice created (manual or auto-batch cron)
Teacher submits structured observation for student
Employee leave request approved by reporting manager
Inventory stock issued to classroom or department
Detailed description with use cases + examples
Marks daily attendance for a class. Supports bulk entries.
Path, query, header params with type + validation
classroomId (path, UUID, required), date (query, ISO date, required)
Schema with required/optional fields, types, examples
Bulk attendance entries: array of { studentId, status, reason? }
All possible error codes with example payloads
Auth schemes (Bearer JWT, API Key), required scopes
Min: 1 success + 1 error example per endpoint
Domain-specific error codes with business meaning
Endpoint-specific rate limit (if different from default)
BRC Rule IDs enforced by this endpoint
Architecture Decision Records impacting this endpoint
Individual functions, pure logic, DTOs, validators, mappers. Mock all dependencies.
Endpoint-level tests with real DB (Dockerized Postgres). Tests happy path + validation + auth + permissions.
Consumer-driven contracts. Consumer defines expected response shape, provider verifies it stays compatible.
Full user journeys across multiple services. Parent login → view child → pay fee → download receipt.
1000 RPS sustained for 10 min. p95 latency < 200ms, error rate < 0.1%.
Final authority on API scope and prioritization
Auth, input validation, output encoding, DPDP compliance
Reviews every new endpoint for security
Reviews payload size, field naming for mobile
Idea raised in governance forum, RFC drafted
Governance Board reviews RFC, approves or rejects
Engineering implements, OpenAPI spec authored alongside code
Deployed to staging, partner testing, feedback collected
Superseded by newer version, sunset date set (12 months out)
End-of-life, returns 410 Gone, migration guide published

Access Control List — permissions attached to an object
Architecture Decision Record — documented architectural choice with rationale
Artificial Intelligence — in PreOne context, LLM-powered features (lesson plan, report card, etc.)
Application Programming Interface — contract between client and server
Apple Push Notification Service — iOS push notifications
Business Rules Catalog — PreOne's 176-rule registry across 12 categories
Business Solution Provider — WhatsApp Business API partner (e.g., Gupshup)
Cross-Origin Resource Sharing — browser security mechanism for cross-domain requests
Time-based job scheduler — used for nightly payroll, invoice batch, etc.
Cross-Site Request Forgery — attack vector mitigated via SameSite cookies + CSRF tokens
Domain-Driven Design — strategic + tactical design methodology for complex domains
Distributed Ledger Technology — in India SMS context, TRAI-mandated template registry
Domain Name System — resolves api.preone.in to load balancer IP
Digital Personal Data Protection Act 2023 — India's data protection law
Data Subject Access Request — user's right to access their PII under DPDP
Data Transfer Object — typed request/response payload structure
Entity Relationship Diagram — database schema documentation
Firebase Cloud Messaging — Android push notifications
Foreign Key — DB constraint linking child table to parent
General Availability — production-ready, fully supported release
Google RPC — high-performance binary protocol (not used in PreOne external API, only internal)
Hypermedia As The Engine Of Application State — REST maturity Level 3 (not enforced in PreOne)
Hash-based Message Authentication Code — used for webhook signing
HyperText Transfer Protocol — PreOne uses 1.1 for REST, WS for real-time
Hardware Security Module — physical device for key management (Vault backend)
Property where same request produces same result — critical for safe retries
Internet Protocol — rate limiting done per IP for anonymous requests
JavaScript Object Notation — sole response format for PreOne APIs
JSON Web Token — stateless auth token (RS256 signed)
Least Recently Used — cache eviction policy (Redis)
Mutual TLS — both client + server authenticate via certs (service-to-service)
Authorization framework — used for parent SSO with Google/Facebook
OpenID Connect — identity layer on OAuth 2.0
Specification format (formerly Swagger) for REST APIs — PreOne uses 3.1
HTTP method for partial resource update
Prevention of Sexual Harassment Act 2013 — workplace safety compliance
HTTP method for creating new resources or triggering actions
Product Requirements Document — PreOne Master PRD v1.0
Post/Redirect/Get — web pattern to prevent double-submit (not used in API context)
HTTP method for full resource replacement
Role-Based Access Control — permissions attached to roles, roles to users
In-memory datastore — used for rate limiting, idempotency cache, sessions
Representational State Transfer — architectural style for HTTP APIs
Remote Procedure Call — action-based API style (PreOne uses REST, not RPC)
Software Development Kit — auto-generated client library from OpenAPI spec
Service Level Objective — internal target (stricter than SLA)
Short Message Service — PreOne uses MSG91 as primary provider
Server-Sent Events — one-way real-time (PreOne uses WebSocket instead, bidirectional)
Transport Layer Security — encrypts all PreOne API traffic (1.3 mandatory)
Time-based One-Time Password — MFA via Google Authenticator
Universally Unique Identifier — PreOne uses v4 for all entity IDs
Web Content Accessibility Guidelines — UI compliance (API exposes alt-text fields)
Full-duplex protocol over TCP — PreOne uses for real-time chat/notifications
Outbound HTTP callback — PreOne notifies partners of events (fee paid, etc.)
Initial draft — Foundation chapters 1–15 + REST API Catalog skeleton
All 30 chapters drafted; WebSocket/Webhook/Events added; sample endpoint cards
Internal review complete; modular split recommendation added; OpenAPI standards finalized
API Freeze — Governance Board sign-off; published to internal docs.preone.in
Auth, DPDP, rate limiting, webhook security validation
Test strategy + contract test coverage validation
176 business rules — every API enforces ≥1 rule
30 BRs + 60 FRs — every FR maps to ≥1 API
8 bounded contexts, 15 aggregates — every API hits 1 aggregate
307 tables — every API payload maps to specific tables
Architecture decisions — every standard references ADR
Frontend specs — every screen consumes specific APIs
Acceptance test IDs — every API has ≥1 acceptance test
